title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Hot and Sour Soup, garnished with green onions and chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with traditional Chinese flavors.

description: This classic Hot and Sour Soup is a symphony of flavors, balancing the fiery kick of chili with the zesty tang of vinegar. Each spoonful offers a delightful texture, packed with tender pork, earthy mushrooms, and silken tofu, making it a perfect starter or light meal. It's a comforting and invigorating dish, beloved for its complex profile that awakens the palate.

ingredients.notes: drained and cut into thin strips
mixed with 6 tbsp cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Slice pork loin into thin strips. Drain and cut tofu into thin strips. Rehydrate dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in hot water for 20 minutes, then stem and slice thinly. Prepare bamboo shoots by julienning if not already. Beat eggs in a small bowl and set aside. Mix cornstarch with cold water to create a slurry.

instructions.title: Cook Pork and Simmer Broth
instructions.text: In a large pot or wok, bring the chicken or vegetable broth to a boil. Add the sliced pork and cook for 2-3 minutes until it changes color. Skim off any foam that rises to the surface.

instructions.title: Add Main Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and tofu to the broth. Bring the soup back to a gentle simmer. Cook for another 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Season the Soup
instructions.text: Stir in the rice vinegar, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, white pepper, and chili oil. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed, adding more vinegar for tang or chili oil for heat. The balance of hot and sour is key.

instructions.title: Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Gradually stir in the cornstarch slurry while the soup is gently simmering. Continue stirring until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. If it's too thick, add a little more broth or water.

instructions.title: Add Egg and Finish
instructions.text: Slowly pour the beaten eggs into the simmering soup in a thin stream while stirring gently with a spoon or ladle in a circular motion. This creates delicate egg ribbons. Turn off the heat. Stir in the sesame oil. Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into bowls, garnish with fresh green onions, and serve immediately.

tips: For authentic flavor, do not skimp on the white pepper and rice vinegar. These are the soul of a good Hot and Sour Soup. Add them gradually and taste to achieve your preferred balance.

If you prefer a vegetarian version, omit the pork and use vegetable broth. You can add extra mushrooms or other vegetables like carrots or snow peas for more substance. Ensure your soy sauce is vegetarian.

Hot and Sour Soup is best enjoyed fresh. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of broth if it has thickened too much.
article:
Hot and Sour Soup is a culinary masterpiece that has captivated palates worldwide, a vibrant testament to the dynamic flavors of Chinese cuisine. Renowned for its unique blend of savory, spicy, and tangy notes, this iconic soup is far more than just an appetizer; it's an experience. Whether you've savored it in a bustling dim sum parlor or a quiet family restaurant, the unforgettable taste often leaves you craving more. This article will guide you through the intricacies of this beloved dish, helping you understand its origins, master its preparation, and even customize it to your liking. If you're searching for the perfect Hot and Sour Soup recipe to recreate that authentic restaurant experience at home, you've come to the right place.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?

At its heart, Hot and Sour Soup is a traditional Chinese soup, most notably associated with Sichuan and Hunan cuisines, though variations exist throughout China and beyond. Its defining characteristics are encapsulated in its name: "hot" refers to the fiery kick, typically from white pepper and chili oil, while "sour" comes from the generous use of rice vinegar. Beyond these primary flavors, the soup boasts a rich umami depth from ingredients like soy sauce, mushrooms, and often pork or chicken broth.

The texture is equally distinctive. It's often thickened slightly with cornstarch, giving it a comforting, velvety consistency. You'll find a delightful medley of ingredients swimming within, including slivers of bamboo shoots, wood ear mushrooms, tofu, and often shredded pork or chicken, all contributing to a satisfying mouthfeel and complex flavor profile. Each spoonful offers a harmonious balance – a warming spice, a refreshing tang, and a savory richness that makes it incredibly addictive. It's a soup that awakens the senses and leaves a lasting impression.

## Ingredients You'll Need

Creating an authentic Hot and Sour Soup recipe at home requires a few key ingredients, many of which are staples in Asian pantries. Here's a comprehensive list to ensure you have everything ready:

*   **For the Broth Base:**
    *   6 cups chicken or vegetable broth (low sodium preferred)
    *   2-3 dried shiitake mushrooms (rehydrated and sliced) – the soaking liquid is also valuable!
*   **Protein & Vegetables:**
    *   4 oz lean pork loin or chicken breast, thinly shredded
    *   4 oz firm tofu, pressed and cut into small matchsticks
    *   1/2 cup bamboo shoots, julienned
    *   1/4 cup wood ear mushrooms (dried, rehydrated and sliced, or fresh), julienned
    *   1 large egg, whisked
*   **Flavor & Seasoning:**
    *   2 tbsp soy sauce (light or regular)
    *   2 tbsp rice vinegar (more to taste)
    *   1 tbsp dark soy sauce (for color, optional)
    *   1-2 tsp white pepper (essential for the "hot" element)
    *   1/2 tsp sesame oil
    *   1 tbsp chili oil (for extra heat, optional)
    *   1-2 cloves garlic, minced (optional)
    *   1/2 inch ginger, grated (optional)
*   **Thickening Slurry:**
    *   2 tbsp cornstarch
    *   4 tbsp cold water
*   **Garnish:**
    *   2 green onions, sliced (green parts only)
    *   Cilantro, chopped (optional)

Having these ingredients on hand is essential for an authentic Hot and Sour Soup recipe that truly shines.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Embarking on this Hot and Sour Soup recipe is a rewarding culinary journey. Follow these steps for a delicious and comforting bowl:

1.  **Prepare Ingredients:** If using dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms, rehydrate them by soaking in hot water for 20-30 minutes. Squeeze out excess water, remove tough stems from shiitake, and slice all mushrooms thinly. Save the shiitake soaking liquid if it's clean; it adds depth. Thinly slice your pork or chicken, julienne bamboo shoots, and cut tofu. Whisk the egg in a small bowl. In another small bowl, combine cornstarch and cold water to make a slurry; set aside.
2.  **Simmer the Broth:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, combine chicken or vegetable broth with the rehydrated shiitake mushrooms (and their strained soaking liquid if using). Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat.
3.  **Add Proteins & Vegetables:** Once simmering, add the shredded pork (or chicken), bamboo shoots, and wood ear mushrooms. Cook for 3-5 minutes until the meat is cooked through and the vegetables are slightly tender.
4.  **Season the Soup:** Stir in the soy sauce, dark soy sauce (if using), white pepper, and optional minced garlic and grated ginger. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. This is where you build the base of your "hot" flavor.
5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give your cornstarch slurry a quick whisk again. While stirring the soup constantly in one direction, slowly pour in the cornstarch slurry until the soup begins to thicken to your desired consistency. You might not need all of it. Continue to cook for another minute to ensure the cornstarch is fully cooked and the soup is clear.
6.  **Add Egg and Vinegar:** Slowly drizzle in the whisked egg in a thin stream while gently stirring the soup in a circular motion. This creates delicate egg "flowers" or ribbons. Immediately after, stir in the rice vinegar and sesame oil. Taste the soup again and adjust the "sour" level with more vinegar if desired, and add chili oil for extra "hotness" if you like.
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with sliced green onions and fresh cilantro (if using). Serve immediately and enjoy!

## Pro Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup

Achieving restaurant-quality Hot and Sour Soup at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks.

*   **Don't Skimp on White Pepper:** This spice is crucial for the authentic "hot" element. While chili oil adds heat, white pepper contributes a unique, pungent warmth that defines the soup. Add it gradually and taste, as its potency can vary.
*   **Balance is Key:** The beauty of this soup lies in the perfect equilibrium of hot, sour, and savory. Always taste and adjust your soy sauce, vinegar, and white pepper. It's easier to add more than to take away.
*   **Quality Broth Matters:** A good quality chicken or vegetable broth forms the foundation. If you have homemade broth, even better.
*   **The Cornstarch Slurry Technique:** Ensure your slurry is well-mixed and add it slowly while stirring the soup. This prevents lumps and allows you to control the thickness precisely. Cook for a minute after adding to remove any starchy taste.
*   **Vinegar Last:** Add the rice vinegar towards the end of the cooking process. Cooking vinegar for too long can diminish its bright, tangy flavor. A fresh splash at the end makes a significant difference.
*   **Fresh Ingredients:** While dried mushrooms are traditional and fantastic for umami, ensure your other ingredients like tofu, bamboo shoots, and protein are fresh for the best texture and flavor.
*   **Customize the Heat:** For more heat, add extra white pepper or a dash of chili oil. For less, reduce the white pepper.

Mastering this Hot and Sour Soup recipe involves understanding these nuances, allowing you to tailor it perfectly to your preference.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Hot and Sour Soup is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways, whether as a standalone dish or part of a larger meal.

*   **As an Appetizer:** This is its most common role. A small bowl before a main course is a fantastic way to awaken the palate. Its bold flavors complement a wide range of Chinese dishes.
*   **Light Lunch:** A larger bowl of Hot and Sour Soup, especially if you've added extra protein or vegetables, can make for a satisfying and warming light lunch. Serve it with a side of plain white rice to soak up the delicious broth.
*   **Pairings with Main Courses:** It pairs wonderfully with classic Chinese stir-fries, noodle dishes, or even roasted meats like Peking duck. The soup's acidity can cut through richer dishes, providing a refreshing contrast.
*   **Alongside Dim Sum:** Hot and Sour Soup is a staple on many dim sum menus. Its vibrant flavor profile is a perfect counterpoint to delicate dumplings, buns, and fried items.
*   **Beverage Pairings:** For drinks, consider a light Chinese tea (like jasmine or oolong) to cleanse the palate, or even a crisp lager or a dry Riesling to complement its complex flavors.

## Variations and Substitutions

One of the great joys of cooking is the ability to adapt a classic Hot and Sour Soup recipe to suit personal tastes, dietary needs, or what you have on hand.

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Easily make this soup meat-free. Simply omit the pork/chicken and use vegetable broth. For added protein, increase the amount of tofu or add rehydrated textured vegetable protein (TVP) or thinly sliced firm mushrooms like king oyster mushrooms. Ensure your soy sauce and chili oil are vegan-friendly.
*   **Protein Alternatives:** Instead of pork or chicken, you could use shrimp, thinly sliced beef, or even fish balls. Add shrimp towards the end of cooking as they cook very quickly.
*   **Mushroom Variety:** Experiment with different types of mushrooms. Shiitake and wood ear are traditional, but enoki, oyster, or even regular button mushrooms can add depth and texture.
*   **Spice Level Adjustment:** For more heat, add extra white pepper, a few dashes of Tabasco or sriracha, or increase the amount of chili oil. For less heat, simply reduce the white pepper.
*   **Sourness Control:** While rice vinegar is standard, you can experiment with a tiny splash of black vinegar for a deeper, more complex sour note. Always add vinegar gradually and taste.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Ensure you use tamari instead of regular soy sauce and verify that your broth and chili oil are gluten-free. Cornstarch is naturally gluten-free.
*   **Egg Drop Style:** If you prefer more distinct egg strands, pour the whisked egg into the soup in a thin stream and stir very gently, almost letting it poach in the hot soup before stirring again.

Armed with this comprehensive Hot and Sour Soup recipe and these adaptable tips, you're now ready to create a truly spectacular bowl of this Chinese classic. It's a dish that offers comfort, excitement, and a burst of flavor in every spoonful – a perfect addition to any home cook's repertoire. Enjoy the process and savor the delicious results!
